growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: ServiceNow now has hard evidence of AI monetization, with its April 6 SEC filing saying Now Assist exceeded $600 million of ACV in 2025 and is on track to top $1 billion in 2026.
growth_picks.analyst_take: ServiceNow looks like a high-quality AI workflow platform trading at a much lower multiple after a 54% drawdown, while growth, FCF, and insider confidence still support a re-rating into earnings.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: FIS is about 43% below its 52-week high yet still profitable with a single-digit forward P/E, and May 5, 2026 earnings are the next catalyst for management to prove the 2026 outlook and post-Worldpay simplification are real, though another margin disappointment would damage the value case.
growth_picks.analyst_take: FIS looks like a cheap post-restructure fintech with real cash flow and insider alignment, provided May earnings confirm the Issuing integration and deleveraging plan.

Microsoft is a high-quality AI and cloud compounder with strong Azure demand, massive contracted backlog, and a near-term earnings catalyst, but the stock is better treated as a disciplined BUY than a STRONG BUY because capex intensity and OpenAI-related execution risk still matter.
KKR looks like a contrarian alt-manager buy after a 39% drawdown because recurring fee earnings and fundraising remain strong, but private-credit liquidity stress keeps it below STRONG BUY.

Intuit is a high-quality software compounder that has rerated sharply despite 17% revenue growth, strong cash generation, accelerating AI/ERP product launches, and a stepped-up buyback, making the current drawdown look more like multiple compression than business deterioration.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: TTD is sitting essentially on its 52-week low and roughly 78% below its 52-week high despite solid profitability, so May 7, 2026 earnings are a clean stop-the-bleeding catalyst after the post-miss washout, but if execution issues persist the stock can stay hated longer than value buyers expect.
growth_picks.analyst_take: TTD looks like a washed-out but still high-quality open-internet ad platform, with strong cash generation, a major CEO open-market buy, and a near-term earnings catalyst, though agency-concentration and execution risk keep this below STRONG BUY.

catalysts_general: Discovery: NTNX is still about 53% below its 52-week high while remaining profitable, and the setup has a real near-term rerating window into its May 27, 2026 earnings report after February's AMD strategic investment and completed $300 million ASR, though another soft cloud/AI spending guide would keep it trapped.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Nutanix remains a profitable, cash-generative VMware-disruption beneficiary with a May 27 earnings catalyst and fresh AMD, NetApp, and buyback support, but near-term revenue timing risk keeps the setup below strong-buy quality.

catalysts_general: Discovery: CoStar qualifies as a high-signal insider setup because the CEO stepped in with a $2.48M buy after a steep drawdown and at least one more executive buy followed, creating a real cluster rather than a token gesture.
growth_picks.analyst_take: A 60% drawdown, confirmed insider buying, a new $1.5 billion repurchase program, and an April 28 earnings catalyst create an attractive setup if management proves Homes.com losses are narrowing on schedule.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Samsara is a real-world AI software monetizer rather than a concept stock because its March 5 results showed ARR rising to about $1.9 billion on a data moat of more than 25 trillion annual data points that now feeds proprietary AI agents for physical operations.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Samsara has a credible physical-operations AI moat with 30% ARR growth, improving profitability, and FY27 GAAP-profit guidance, though the stock still carries premium-multiple and insider-sale risk.

H&R Block screens as a beaten-down cash compounder with cheap earnings, a double-digit FCF yield, and a near-term tax-season earnings catalyst, though regulatory and execution risks keep it below top-tier conviction.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Pinterest became a news-driven idea after Elliott injected $1B and management paired it with an unusually aggressive repurchase program, creating a clear capital-allocation catalyst in a still-discounted internet name.
growth_picks.analyst_take: High-margin, cash-rich ad platform trading far below prior highs looks mispriced relative to its buyback-backed share shrink and ongoing AI/CTV monetization buildout, though retail ad softness keeps this out of STRONG BUY territory.
Gen Digital is a cash-rich, guidance-raising consumer cyber-safety platform trading 41% below its 52-week high, but leverage and MoneyLion integration risks cap conviction.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Lululemon is deeply hated after a brutal rerating, yet the business still has strong international growth, meaningful buybacks, and a balance sheet that gives it time to recover.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Lululemon is statistically cheap for a premium brand and still generates strong cash, but March 17, 2026 guidance confirmed North America weakness, gross-margin pressure, and leadership/governance noise that keep the rerating case unfinished.

Qualcomm is a cash-rich, reasonably valued semiconductor franchise, but near-term handset memory constraints and the longer-term Apple modem insourcing risk limit upside until April earnings revalidate the diversification story.

POET has credible AI-optics product momentum and a much stronger cash runway after its January financing, but commercialization is still too early and too diluted to justify chasing the stock before clearer revenue conversion.

High-quality aerospace aftermarket compounder with fresh insider buying and raised 2026 guidance, but the stock still looks too expensive for a clean BUY after debt-funded acquisitions and limited confirmed near-term catalysts.

Frontline has strong near-term earnings support from contracted VLCC charters and expected Q1 asset-sale gains, but with the stock already near its 52-week high and leverage plus newbuild capex still elevated, the upside no longer looks mispriced.
Coupang is stabilizing after the breach shock and has credible insider support, but the stock still looks too expensive for a low-margin retailer with unresolved regulatory and competitive pressure.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Nike looks like a classic oversold quality reset because the stock has been pushed to fresh multi-year lows even though the brand remains elite and the current pressure is tied to an operational cleanup rather than balance-sheet stress.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Nike is an oversold quality franchise with early North America and performance-category improvement, but the turnaround still lacks enough earnings visibility to justify a fresh BUY at roughly 22x forward earnings.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Vertiv paired a February surge in AI-driven orders and a $15 billion backlog with an April 4 plan to spend $50 million expanding Ohio cooling capacity, which is a concrete signal that liquid-cooling demand from AI racks is still outrunning supply.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Vertiv is a real AI infrastructure winner with strong orders, backlog, and cooling-capacity expansion, but the stock already trades near highs on a stretched multiple that leaves limited margin of safety into earnings.

catalysts_general: Discovery: CrowdStrike's board added another $500 million to its repurchase authorization on April 6, 2026, lifting the total to $1.5 billion after already buying back 413,130 shares for $150.6 million at an average $364.57, which reads as management buying into a valuation disconnect after a strong fiscal year.
growth_picks.analyst_take: CrowdStrike remains a high-quality cyber platform with record ARR growth, strong free cash flow, and a fresh $1.5 billion buyback, but at roughly 69x forward earnings and only about 25% off its 52-week high, the valuation still leaves limited room for error.

catalysts_general: Discovery: NVIDIA's March 31 strategic investment pulled Marvell directly into NVLink Fusion while Marvell's completed Celestial AI acquisition adds photonic-fabric leverage, making it a stronger picks-and-shovels beneficiary of custom AI systems and optical scale-up.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Marvell has a credible AI custom-silicon and interconnect growth story, but at roughly its 52-week high with only modest upside to consensus targets, the risk/reward is no longer attractive enough to chase.

catalysts_general: Discovery: IperionX posted a four-insider buying cluster around March 27, 2026: CEO Anastasios Arima bought about $1.33 million, Executive Chair Todd Hannigan about $498.2K, President Toby Symonds about $262.6K, and director Tony Tripeny about $99.5K, or roughly $2.19 million combined backing the U.S. titanium supply-chain story.
growth_picks.analyst_take: U.S. government backing, fresh insider cluster buying, and near-term Tennessee/defense milestones support the long-term titanium thesis, but zero revenue and heavy cash burn keep IperionX too speculative to upgrade beyond HOLD today.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Eaton is monetizing AI power scarcity with a January 28 modular data-center launch that cuts deployment schedules by about 35% on average and a February earnings release showing Electrical Americas orders accelerating on data-center momentum.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Eaton is a real AI-electrification winner with strong orders and portfolio catalysts, but the stock is too close to its highs and too expensive relative to upside to justify a fresh buy here.

catalysts_general: Discovery: NATO's March 26, 2026 spending report and Europe's current Patriot and air-defense gap after missile reallocations to the Middle East point to sustained demand for RTX's Patriot, GEM-T and AMRAAM franchises, with production already being expanded under February 2026 Pentagon framework deals.
growth_picks.analyst_take: RTX has real aerospace and missile-demand strength with record backlog and rising 2026 cash-flow guidance, but the stock is already near its high and the remaining Pratt & Whitney GTF overhang limits fresh-buy upside.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Washington is still hardening critical-minerals policy against Chinese leverage, and MP is the clearest U.S.-listed direct beneficiary because it owns Mountain Pass and is ramping U.S. magnet production just as rare-earth and permanent-magnet supply security is being treated as national security infrastructure.
growth_picks.analyst_take: MP Materials is the clearest U.S. rare-earth supply-chain winner, but negative free cash flow, a rich forward multiple, and magnet-ramp execution risk keep it in HOLD rather than BUY territory.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Pictet Asset Management disclosed a Schedule 13D stake of 7,618,863 shares, or 6.35% of FBIN, representing roughly half a billion dollars built during housing-sector pessimism and signaling intent to engage rather than passive ownership.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cheap on forward earnings and supported by cash flow, but leadership turmoil and still-weak housing demand keep Fortune Brands in prove-it mode despite activist pressure.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Symbotic looks like a credible physical-AI second derivative because on March 31 it won a major new AWG warehouse automation project expected to process nearly 19 million cases annually, reinforcing that AI-enabled robotics is still landing large-scale supply-chain deployments.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Symbotic is executing and landing real warehouse-automation wins, but the current setup still looks too expensive and too concentrated in Walmart-related risk to justify a fresh buy.

catalysts_general: Discovery: AVOID - Barclays downgraded LegalZoom in March over AI disintermediation risk, and the February legal-tech selloff after Anthropic's legal tooling launch showed how exposed low-complexity document and research workflows are to commoditization.
growth_picks.analyst_take: LegalZoom is generating real cash and adding AI distribution catalysts, but the core bear case that AI commoditizes low-complexity legal workflows is still unresolved, so the stock remains a show-me story into May earnings.

catalysts_general: Discovery: MGM combines depressed sentiment with repeated major-owner buying and a fresh governance agreement that may lower overhang while investors wait for BetMGM and Strip upside.
growth_picks.analyst_take: MGM has credible near-term catalysts from BetMGM cash generation, Macau strength, and IAC's continued support, but with shares near the 52-week high, leverage still extreme, and Las Vegas Strip trends soft, the risk/reward is not attractive enough for a fresh BUY.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Nuclear fuel security is hardening into a geopolitical procurement theme, and Cameco has a direct revenue lock-in after signing a multi-year India supply deal while Kazakhstan's tighter state control over uranium ground raises the scarcity value of western-owned supply.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cameco remains a high-quality nuclear-fuel franchise with real contract and Westinghouse catalysts, but the stock already prices in much of that upside at a very rich multiple.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Dan Loeb's Third Point boosted its Rocket position by 138.27% in the latest 13F cycle to about 9.51 million shares worth roughly $178.5 million, a meaningful size-up into a volatile but liquid large-cap mortgage name.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Rocket is executing well on Redfin and Mr. Cooper integration and gaining mortgage share, but renewed rate pressure and acquisition risk keep the stock in the hold bucket.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Western supply-security premiums for controlled rare earths are widening, and Energy Fuels' late-March terbium oxide milestone gives it one of the few U.S.-listed paths to monetize domestic heavy rare-earth processing scarcity.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Energy Fuels has credible U.S. uranium and heavy rare earth catalysts, but the stock already discounts a lot of future strategic success despite negative free cash flow, shrinking revenue, and execution-heavy expansion plans.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: PayPal remains about 42% below its 52-week high despite strong earnings power and cash generation, and its May 5, 2026 earnings report is the next hard catalyst for branded checkout and margin-progress re-rating, although the bear case is that growth can stay too sluggish for multiple expansion.
growth_picks.analyst_take: PayPal is a cheap, cash-generative payments franchise with real May earnings and product-launch catalysts, but weak branded-checkout trends and a fresh CEO reset keep it in HOLD until execution improves.

Accenture is a cash-rich, de-rated services compounder with credible AI demand, but federal exposure, slower discretionary consulting spend, and unresolved regulatory overhangs limit near-term rerating.
High-quality payroll and HCM franchise trading well below its highs, but upside is capped by Paycor-driven leverage and only modest small-business demand.

Alamos Gold has strong North American asset quality, record 2025 cash generation, and credible multi-year production growth, but the stock is only about 14% below its 52-week high and still faces a heavy 2026 execution and capex burden.

Diamondback is a high-quality Permian cash-flow machine with active buybacks and a near-term debt optimization catalyst, but the stock is only about 9% off its 52-week high and recent insider/holder activity leans toward selling rather than conviction buying.
Synopsys has real Ansys-integration and AI product catalysts, but at roughly 24x forward earnings with China and Design IP headwinds, the risk/reward is not compelling enough for a fresh buy.

Strong FY25 user growth, cash generation, and a new $400M buyback are real positives, but the 2026 guidance reset, AI-driven competition risk, and recent insider selling keep DUOL from being a fresh buy before May 4 earnings.
Deep-value optics and deleveraging are real, but withdrawn FY26 guidance and a weak Q3 outlook keep Coty in prove-it mode despite insider buying and strategic-review optionality.
EchoStar is a leveraged regulatory-arbitrage story trading near its highs despite shrinking revenue, negative free cash flow, and heavy dependence on deal execution rather than operating improvement.
Mixed Phase 3 myelofibrosis data and a strained balance sheet leave Karyopharm dependent on more financing before its next meaningful catalyst.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Plug is still speculative, but it now has the kind of hated-turning setup that matters because gross margin finally flipped positive and management laid out a clearer path to EBITDA breakeven in late 2026.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Operational improvement is real, but Plug still looks too capital-constrained and dilution-prone to justify a long entry above consensus fair value.
